entry.text: Cool stone and the sweet smell of malt. The crowned cask waits among the racks.

look.say: Stairs back up to the common room.

use.say: You climb back up to the warmth.

look.say: Bare shelves. Nothing useful here — the ale is in the casks.
The royal cask still holds plenty. Your tankard is already full.
You already carry a tankard of royal ale.
A fine cask marked with a crown. Your empty tankard would fit under the spigot.
A cask banded in brass, a tiny crown burned into the wood. Royal ale — if you had a tankard.

use.say: You need an empty tankard. There's one on the bar upstairs.
You draw a perfect pour of royal ale — gold, foam, and a king's promise.

tankard.say: You draw a perfect pour of royal ale — gold, foam, and a king's promise.

flour.say: Flour and ale don't mix. Bake upstairs.

loaf.say: Bread doesn't belong on a spigot.

notice.say: The notice sent you here. Fill a tankard.

look.say: Common ale for common nights. The crowned cask is the one that matters.

use.say: Save those for the regulars. The King wants the crowned cask.

take.say: Too heavy, and the wrong brew.

objectives.text: Goal: bake a golden loaf (flour in the courtyard, oven upstairs)
Goal: use the tankard with the crowned cask
Goal: bring an empty tankard from the bar upstairs
